Bloomberg.heading: JPMorgan Analysts See AI Disruption Spurring Small Bank Mergers
Bloomberg.summary:
Analysts at JPMorgan Chase, led by Vivek Juneja, highlighted that smaller U.S. banks struggling to match larger peers' artificial intelligence (AI) investments may face pressure to merge in order to mitigate revenue impacts. Most banks are increasing AI spending to stay competitive, but uncertainty about AI’s effect on earnings has contributed to a recent sector-wide stock selloff. An index of U.S. bank stocks fell 5.5% last week, compared to a 1.4% decline in the S&P 500.

Juneja noted that larger money center banks and some regional lenders, which can invest more heavily in AI, are better positioned for potential benefits, though concrete evidence of AI’s impact on revenue remains limited. Financial stocks have broadly declined amid investor concerns over AI-driven disruptions to business areas such as wealth management and investment banking—key revenue sources for bigger banks, which have underperformed regional banks this year.

Despite these challenges, Juneja expects larger money center banks to offset AI-related risks in the near term through increased trading revenues, and maintain long-term AI investment capacity to mitigate negative effects. While mid-sized and larger regionals are not currently expected to sell assets, the analysts warned of potential underinvestment risks among some lenders.

Bloomberg.heading: Apple Ramps Up Work on Glasses, Pendant, and Camera AirPods for AI Era
Bloomberg.summary:
Apple Inc. is accelerating development of three new wearable devices focused on artificial intelligence, including smart glasses, a camera-equipped pendant, and enhanced AirPods. These devices will integrate the Siri digital assistant, using visual context to perform tasks, and will connect to the iPhone. The smart glasses, code-named N50, are designed as an advanced product with high-resolution and computer vision cameras, allowing real-time environmental understanding and AI interaction without a display. Production could begin as early as December 2024, with a potential release in 2027. The pendant and AI-enhanced AirPods will have simpler, lower-resolution cameras aimed at complementing AI functions rather than capturing photos or videos.

Apple is focusing on in-house frame design for the glasses, using premium materials and embedding components within the frame. The glasses aim to offer features like identifying objects, reading printed text into digital form, context-aware reminders, and navigation cues referencing real-world landmarks. The pendant, about the size of an AirTag, can be worn on clothing or as a necklace and acts as an always-on camera and microphone for Siri, relying heavily on the iPhone for processing. Its release, if approved, could be as soon as next year.

Apple plans to introduce improved AI capabilities in Siri with iOS 27 this year, including a chatbot-like interface developed in collaboration with Google. The company is advancing AI integration following challenges in upgrading Siri and is investing in new technologies to expand user interaction beyond the iPhone.

Apple’s efforts follow industry trends, with competitors like Meta and OpenAI investing in AI-powered wearables. Apple is aiming to differentiate through build quality and camera technology, seeking to strengthen its ecosystem amid mixed reception of previous ventures like the Vision Pro headset. Future products may include smart glasses with augmented reality displays, but such launches are years away. Apple is also developing AI-enabled home devices, including smart displays, HomePod updates, and indoor sensors for security and automation.

Bloomberg.heading: BofA Survey Shows Investor Worry Over Capex Race at Record High
Bloomberg.summary:
According to Bank of America Corp.’s latest fund manager survey, a record 35% of investors believe companies are overinvesting, marking the highest level in two decades. Despite this concern, investors are the most bullish on global corporate earnings since August 2021. The survey highlights expectations of record capital expenditure in 2024, with the four largest US tech firms forecasting about $650 billion in spending by 2026. Market reactions have varied: Microsoft shares dropped sharply over concerns regarding investment payback, while Meta Platforms rose significantly due to its AI investment plans.

A quarter of survey participants identified an "AI bubble" as the top market risk, and 30% view tech capital spending on AI as a potential trigger for a credit crisis. Investors are currently reducing exposure to technology stocks and the US dollar while increasing allocations to energy, materials, and consumer staples. The reduction in tech stock exposure is the most significant since March 2025, with net overweight in the sector dropping from 19% to 5% within a month. Additionally, there is a notable rotation from US stocks toward emerging and European markets, reaching levels last seen in early 2021. The survey also noted small-cap stocks outperforming, with respondents overweight small caps relative to large caps for the first time since April 2021.

The Bank of America Global Fund Manager Survey was conducted from February 6 to 12, including 162 participants managing $440 billion in assets.

Bloomberg.heading: Goldman Plans to Remove DEI Criteria for Board Members, WSJ Says
Bloomberg.summary:
Goldman Sachs Group Inc. plans to remove diversity criteria, such as race, gender identity, and sexual orientation, from the factors used to identify potential board candidates. This consideration follows a request from the National Legal and Policy Center, a nonprofit critical of diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) policies. Goldman Sachs is expected to approve these changes soon, altering the board nomination process that previously included diversity factors. The bank’s board currently comprises 14 members, including five women, one multiracial, and one Asian member.

This move is part of a broader trend among major Wall Street banks to roll back DEI initiatives, influenced in part by former President Donald Trump’s administration, which pushed companies to abandon such programs viewed as discriminatory. Citigroup removed diversity goals and interview requirements for diverse candidates last year, while Bank of America and Wells Fargo have also scaled back DEI efforts. Goldman Sachs has already eliminated certain diversity targets from key regulatory filings and ended a commitment to reject IPO business from companies with all White, male boards.

The reduction in DEI emphasis began after an executive order requiring federal contractors to terminate such policies, a mandate recently upheld by a U.S. federal appellate court. According to Bloomberg Intelligence, average shareholder support for DEI-related proposals dropped to 8% in 2025 from 14% the previous year and 36% in 2021.

Bloomberg.heading: Google Secures Ormat Geothermal Power for Nevada Data Centers
Bloomberg.summary:
Alphabet Inc.’s Google has entered a power purchase agreement with geothermal energy company Ormat Technologies to supply up to 150 megawatts of renewable power for its data centers in Nevada. The power will be provided through Google’s existing agreement with NV Energy, a Nevada utility owned by Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Ormat will generate the electricity from a series of new geothermal projects currently under development, with commercial operations expected between 2028 and 2030. The agreement will last for 15 years following the commissioning of the final project. 

Ormat’s stock price rose by as much as 8.1% intraday, marking the company’s largest gain since 2023. Analysts from Oppenheimer & Co. identified Ormat as a potential key player in supplying energy for AI infrastructure demands.

Geothermal power provides a reliable, around-the-clock renewable energy source, which is beneficial for large data center operators like Google seeking to achieve emissions targets while addressing concerns over energy storage and grid reliability. Google’s head of energy market innovation, Briana Kobor, stated that the agreement uses a cost-covering framework that protects other utility customers and enhances the stability of the local power grid.

The deal awaits approval from Nevada’s public utilities commission.

Bloomberg.heading: Home Depot Gets Stricter on Bonuses Amid Housing Market Freeze
Bloomberg.summary: Home Depot Inc. is tightening bonus payout criteria for managers amid a business slowdown. The company will raise the minimum sales performance threshold from 90% to 95%, with eligible employees meeting this target now receiving 25% of the bonus instead of 50%, according to a memo seen by Bloomberg. These changes, aligning incentives with pre-pandemic standards, will affect bonuses paid in September. Home Depot maintains a separate profit-sharing program for all staff. The company attributed the adjustments to a need to reward strong performance and enhance accountability, reversing bonus leniency introduced during the pandemic to address wider financial result variations. This move reflects a broader trend of companies tightening labor policies as pandemic-era pressures ease, with 2025 projected job additions at the lowest since 2003, and recent job cuts at their highest January level since 2009. Home Depot faces operational challenges including a frozen housing market slowing business activity, increasing affordability concerns, and disruptions from raids by federal agents at some store parking lots, although the company states these have not materially affected business and denies coordination with immigration authorities. In response to market conditions, Home Depot recently implemented job cuts across teams and mandated a five-day office attendance policy for corporate employees starting in April. The company is scheduled to report its quarterly earnings later this month.

Bloomberg.heading: Teslas Austin Robotaxis Report 14 Crashes in First Eight Months
Bloomberg.summary:
Tesla Inc.’s robotaxi service in Austin has been involved in at least 14 crash incidents since its launch in June, according to reports submitted to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). The service started with around a dozen vehicles accompanied by human safety monitors, gradually increasing its fleet size. The first reported crash occurred in July and initially was said to involve only property damage; later updates revealed minor injuries and hospitalization resulted. Several other incidents involved minor injuries, collisions with city buses, and backing into objects in parking lots.

In January, Tesla began offering some fully driverless rides in a limited number of Austin vehicles, though it is unclear if any crashes involved these unmanned rides. Details on these incidents are sparse, as Tesla often redacts narrative descriptions, with most reports citing only property damage. Austin remains Tesla’s only city with active robotaxi service; Tesla also operates a rideshare service in the San Francisco Bay Area using human drivers.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk has emphasized autonomy and robotics, despite the core electric vehicle business facing challenges. Tesla has not disclosed the current size of its Austin robotaxi fleet but mentioned a combined total of 500 rideshare vehicles between Austin and the Bay Area in January.

By comparison, Alphabet’s Waymo, operating around 2,500 vehicles across several U.S. cities including Austin, has reported hundreds of incidents since June, with approximately 50 in Austin alone. Tesla aims to expand its robotaxi service to about seven additional cities by midyear but has missed previous expansion timelines. Waymo began offering public driverless rides in Phoenix in 2020 and remains ahead in autonomous ride service scale.

WSJ.heading: Can Nvidia’s stock approach $400? Here’s why earnings may mark the start of a big rally.
WSJ.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse advised Nvidia investors to remain calm amid a lack of momentum in the stock this year. Muse highlighted that Nvidia is entering a "rack-scale super cycle," driven by the increasing adoption of its Blackwell artificial-intelligence chips and related rack systems. The analyst also mentioned the anticipated launch of the Rubin family of products expected in the second half of the year, which could potentially boost the company’s performance. These developments suggest upcoming growth opportunities for Nvidia as it expands its AI hardware offerings.

WSJ.heading: Infosys Stock Is Rising. It Just Unveiled This AI Partnership With Anthropic.
WSJ.summary: Infosys announced a collaboration with Anthropic to develop custom artificial intelligence (AI) agents. Following this news, Infosys shares experienced a significant increase in trading value. The partnership aims to leverage Anthropic's AI expertise to enhance Infosys's service offerings in the technology sector. As an Indian IT services and consulting firm, Infosys seeks to strengthen its position within the rapidly evolving AI landscape. This move is part of the company's broader strategy to address market expectations and investor concerns regarding its adaptability to emerging technological trends. The collaboration with Anthropic is intended to accelerate Infosys's capabilities in AI development and deployment for its clientele.

WSJ.heading: Nvidia Stock Falls. There Are Reasons to Believe It Will Rally.
WSJ.summary: Nvidia's stock declined slightly early Tuesday. However, a Wall Street analyst provided a positive outlook on the company. Additionally, Nvidia reported strong efficiency metrics for its artificial-intelligence chips, which may offer confidence to investors.

WSJ.heading: Resilient U.S. Oil Production Is a Boon to Trump. How Long Will It Last?
WSJ.summary:
U.S. oil production has defied expectations of decline despite a recent drop in crude prices, reaching record levels of 13.6 million barrels per day last year, exceeding forecasts by the Energy Information Administration. This resilience is attributed to technological advancements, consolidation within the industry, and a shift toward larger oil companies with stronger financial positions, particularly in the Permian Basin, which produces about half of the country's crude. These companies employ innovative drilling techniques, such as extended horizontal wells and improved fracking methods, enabling increased output at lower costs.

While smaller, debt-reliant frackers have exited the market, major producers like ExxonMobil, Chevron, ConocoPhillips, and Diamondback now dominate production and investment decisions. These companies have improved efficiency, drilling faster and using fewer rigs and crews while boosting production. Despite these gains, many prime drilling areas have matured, requiring development of lower-quality formations and new extraction technologies to sustain output levels.

Analysts predict U.S. oil production will plateau this year and eventually decline around 2027 as resource depletion continues. Some regions like North Dakota’s Bakken Shale have already seen reduced activity. Industry leaders acknowledge potential advances in recovery technology but caution that rising production costs and fewer smaller producers may limit supply growth in the future. The Biden administration notes ongoing efforts to improve extraction technologies, but sustained long-term increases in shale output may be challenging.

WSJ.heading: Spain to Probe X, Meta, TikTok Over AI Images
WSJ.summary:
The Spanish government has requested prosecutors to investigate social media platforms X, Meta, and TikTok for potential crimes involving AI-generated images depicting minors, including nude images. This move follows the reported emergence of approximately 3 million AI-generated nude images, some featuring minors, online within two weeks. Spanish Minister Elma Saiz emphasized the need to prevent such content from being amplified by algorithms, citing child safety concerns. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez publicly called for the probe, alleging that these platforms undermine children’s mental health, dignity, and rights.

Meta stated it enforces strict policies against child sexual exploitation and nonconsensual intimate imagery, employing AI tools designed to refuse inappropriate content generation, but declined comment on the investigation. X did not respond directly, but previously claimed to restrict AI-generated imagery through its Grok AI tool and maintains zero tolerance for child exploitation and nonconsensual nudity. TikTok also did not comment but states its policies prohibit content related to the sexual abuse or exploitation of minors.

Advances in AI allow users to generate images via text prompts, raising concerns when safeguards are insufficient to block explicit requests. X’s Grok AI faces increased scrutiny in Europe after generating sexualized deepfake images, leading to an EU investigation over potential violations of the Digital Services Act and an inquiry by Ireland’s data protection authority.

Spain’s investigation request follows Prime Minister Sanchez’s proposal to ban social media access for children under 16 through age-verification systems, aligning Spain with countries like Australia implementing similar measures.

WSJ.heading: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
WSJ.summary:
Barclays analysts forecast the physical AI market—including humanoid robots, autonomous vehicles, industrial automation, and drones—could reach $0.5 trillion to $1.4 trillion by 2035, with autonomous vehicles contributing nearly half the growth, up to $550 billion. China currently leads global robot deployment, accounting for over 85% of new humanoid robot installations in 2025, compared to 13% in the U.S.

Wedbush analysts report that Apple is overcoming recent AI challenges and is set to launch significant AI developments this year, supported by its partnership with Google Gemini and new AI leadership. They view the recent stock price decline as unwarranted and estimate AI monetization could boost Apple’s share value by $75 to $100 in the coming years.

Warner Bros. Discovery has reopened talks with Paramount Skydance to improve its acquisition offer, currently set at $31 per share, conditional on negotiations. However, Warner maintains Netflix’s offer is superior and remains committed to that deal, noting unresolved issues with Paramount’s proposal. Netflix has granted Warner a seven-day waiver to negotiate with Paramount.

The Bank of America February global fund manager survey indicates gold remains the most crowded trade for the second month in a row, with 50% of fund managers favoring long gold positions, slightly down from 51% in January. Additionally, 20% identify buying major U.S. tech stocks (Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, Tesla) as crowded trades. 

In Europe, healthcare and banking sectors have gained favor among fund managers for February, replacing technology as the most overweight sectors. Despite remaining net bullish on technology following its January prominence, exposure has been reduced amid a software sector selloff. Technology is also considered the most overvalued sector in Europe, while autos are the most underweighted, with nearly 60% of fund managers allocating less to this sector than the benchmark.

WSJ.heading: This auto-parts maker is jumping on the breakup bandwagon. Here’s why the stock is tanking.
WSJ.summary: Shares of Genuine Parts Company (GPC) fell sharply on Tuesday following a significant miss on quarterly profit expectations and a weaker-than-anticipated outlook for 2026. The disappointing financial results were partly attributed to softness in its domestic NAPA auto-parts segment. Despite the negative earnings news, Genuine Parts announced plans to spin off its business into two separate publicly traded companies after an extensive review. One company will focus on automotive parts, including the NAPA business, while the other will concentrate on industrial parts, featuring the Motion brand. Investors reacted negatively to the profit shortfall and outlook miss, overshadowing the strategic decision to split the company.

WSJ.heading: Warner Bros. and Paramount Restart Merger Talks. Why Netflix Agreed.
WSJ.summary: Warner Bros. Discovery has resumed negotiations with Paramount Skydance, allowing CBS's parent company a seven-day period to present its best and final offer.

WSJ.heading: Warner Reopens Talks With Paramount After Sweetened Offer
WSJ.summary:
Warner Bros. Discovery announced it will resume deal discussions with Paramount following Paramount's improved $31-per-share offer, up from $30, contingent on Warner’s agreement to negotiate. Paramount last week sweetened its all-cash $77.9 billion hostile bid for Warner, aiming to acquire the entire company, including cable channels CNN and TNT. Warner’s move to engage Paramount sets the stage for a potential bidding contest with Netflix, which holds a $72 billion all-cash agreement to acquire Warner’s movie and TV studios and HBO Max streaming service. Netflix retains the right to match any competing offer and has granted Warner a seven-day waiver to engage with Paramount.

Paramount’s offer includes covering Warner’s $2.8 billion termination fee payable to Netflix if the deal fails, plus a “ticking fee” of 25 cents per share per quarter to Warner shareholders starting January 2027. Warner's board expects Paramount’s final bid to exceed $31 per share. Despite reopening talks with Paramount, Warner continues to prefer Netflix’s offer, citing its superior value, regulatory clarity, and shareholder protections, with a shareholder vote on the Netflix deal scheduled for March 20.

Concerns remain within Warner about Paramount’s debt load and covenants, which might impede operations before closing, unlike Netflix's agreement. Paramount has repeatedly submitted unsolicited bids since last year, aiming to acquire the full company as Warner plans to spin off cable networks into a separate entity. Paramount has criticized Warner’s process as unfair, while Netflix calls Paramount’s bid disputes a distraction.

The Justice Department is reviewing both deals, investigating potential antitrust issues including whether Netflix engaged in anticompetitive behavior. Netflix denies any such investigation beyond standard regulatory review. Investor activity has increased, with Ancora Holdings recently acquiring a stake to urge Warner to negotiate with Paramount.

Reuters.heading: Adage Capital trims stakes in AI heavyweights
Reuters.summary: Adage Capital Partners reduced its holdings in several major AI-related companies during the fourth quarter of 2025, including Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Nvidia, according to its latest SEC filing. However, Adage increased its stake in Oracle by approximately 19%, to 1.87 million shares valued at around $365 million. Meanwhile, Softbank Group disclosed it fully divested its $5.8 billion position in Nvidia in October, a move initially reported in November, to fund further investments in OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT. Nvidia's share price has declined about 7% since Softbank's sale announcement on November 11. Nvidia is scheduled to report its quarterly earnings on February 25, with analysts expecting a 67% revenue increase. Both Adage’s and Softbank’s filings, which report holdings as of December 31 compared to September 30, reflect positions at the end of 2025 but do not include changes made in the first quarter of 2026. Investor concerns persist over whether heavy investments in AI technologies will yield returns to support current high valuations after a multi-year rally fueled by AI optimism.

Reuters.heading: Berkshire-owned PacifiCorp, citing liquidity, sells Washington assets to Portland General Electric for $1.9 billion
Reuters.summary:
PacifiCorp, a utility owned by Berkshire Hathaway, is selling wind, natural gas generation, and distribution assets in central and southern Washington state to Portland General Electric (PGE) for $1.9 billion, citing liquidity concerns amid ongoing wildfire litigation in Oregon. The sale includes the Chehalis natural gas plant, Goodnoe Hills and Marengo I and II wind facilities, and 4,500 miles of transmission and distribution lines. PGE will acquire approximately 140,000 customers across 2,700 square miles in Washington. Manulife Investment Management will hold a 49% stake in the acquired Washington utility business. The transaction requires federal and state regulatory approval and is expected to take at least one year to complete. 

PacifiCorp faces potential damages claims of up to $52 billion from lawsuits alleging its power lines caused four wildfires in Oregon in 2020; trials may continue through 2028. PacifiCorp has appealed to have a class action status revoked and to limit liability for emotional distress claims. The utility cited "diverging policies" across the six western states it serves as a factor pressuring its financial stability and credit ratings. 

The sale excludes PacifiCorp’s hydroelectric facilities in Washington. As industrial and data center electricity demand rises, utilities are seeking new generation and transmission assets. PGE described the acquired assets as a valuable mix of natural gas and wind resources providing safe and affordable power. In its latest financial report, PGE posted an adjusted fourth-quarter profit of $53 million, below analyst expectations.

Reuters.heading: Exxon committed to moving fast to develop gas in Guyana, executive says
Reuters.summary: Exxon Mobil is committed to rapidly developing natural gas projects in Guyana, according to the company's upstream chief, Dan Ammann. This commitment aligns with the Guyanese government's goal to increase natural gas output and usage, including applications in petrochemical plants and potential data centers. Last year, Exxon introduced the "Wales Gas Vision," an initial plan to supply gas for onshore petrochemical and power projects, but progress has been limited since. Ammann highlighted the complexity of natural gas projects compared to oil, noting the need for collaboration with multiple stakeholders such as public utilities, communities, and regulators. Exxon has completed a pipeline ready to deliver gas, pending the completion of power plants expected later this year. The company is prepared to advance engineering, execution, and investment as government approvals and market frameworks develop.

Reuters.heading: My News
Reuters.summary: Please provide the financial news article you want summarized.

Reuters.heading: Iraq approves 'amicable settlement' with Russia's Lukoil over transfer of operations of giant field
Reuters.summary: On February 17, the Iraqi cabinet approved an amicable settlement with Russia's Lukoil regarding the transfer of operations of the West Qurna 2 oil field to the state-run Basra Oil Company. The field, one of the world's largest, produces about 0.5% of global oil and nearly 10% of Iraq's output. Iraq nationalized West Qurna 2 last month following U.S. sanctions on Lukoil aimed at pressuring Russia over the Ukraine conflict. Lukoil must sell its assets by February 28 under these sanctions. Concurrently, Iraq is in discussions with U.S. oil major Chevron to improve contractual terms for involvement in the field, a deal that requires cabinet approval. Chevron's participation would expand its presence in Iraq after recent acquisitions, including its $53 billion purchase of Hess set to complete in 2025. Meanwhile, Basra Oil Company is managing West Qurna 2 operations temporarily for 12 months amid ongoing ownership negotiations.

Reuters.heading: Rousey to make MMA return after a decade to fight Carano
Reuters.summary: Former UFC champion Ronda Rousey will return to mixed martial arts after ten years to fight Gina Carano on May 16. The featherweight bout, set at 145 pounds, will take place at the Intuit Dome in Los Angeles and will be streamed worldwide on Netflix at no extra cost. The fight will be sanctioned under the Unified Rules of MMA and consist of five five-minute rounds inside a hexagon cage. Rousey, 39, holds a professional MMA record of 12 wins and 2 losses, including nine submissions and three knockouts. She was the UFC's first female bantamweight champion, an Olympic judo medalist, a former WWE star, and the first woman inducted into the UFC Hall of Fame. Carano, 43, has a 7-1 record and is recognized for popularizing women's MMA, earning the nickname "Face of Women's MMA." The fight was initiated by Rousey, who approached Carano to make the matchup happen, with Carano stating it had been Rousey's dream to fight her. Both fighters have also appeared in Hollywood, including roles in the "Fast & Furious" franchise.
